top_signals.title: ECB Ready to Hike Again in July If Necessary, Nagel Says
top_signals.summary: The European Central Bank is prepared to raise interest rates for a second straight meeting next month if the shock from the war in the Middle East requires it, according to Governing Council member Joachim Nagel.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Oil Seen Rising Past $150 If Hormuz Still Closed
top_signals.summary: FGE NexantECA Chairman Emeritus Fereidun Fesharaki says he sees oil prices rising to $150 a barrel if the Strait of Hormuz is still effectively closed by August. On Bloomberg's Insight with Haslinda Amin, he also said oil could be closer to $200 a barrel by the end of the year. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: What energy insiders in DC are saying about oil prices and a possible Iran deal - CNBC
top_signals.summary: What energy insiders in DC are saying about oil prices and a possible Iran deal  CNBC
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: BlackRock files 8-A for yield-bearing bitcoin ETF; analyst expects launch next week
top_signals.summary: Bloomberg Analyst Eric Balchunas said the filing signals an imminent launch, adding that he expects it to start trading next week.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: SpaceX raising $75 billion in record-setting IPO as Nasdaq debut awaits
top_signals.summary: SpaceX is selling 555.6 shares at $135 a piece, raising $75 billion in the largest IPO on record.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Danske Bank CEO Eyes First Acquisition Since Laundering Crisis
top_signals.summary: Danske Bank A/S Chief Executive Officer Carsten Egeriis says it’s time to start thinking about acquisitions, potentially ending a nearly decade-long hiatus from major deals for Denmark’s largest lender.
